title: Iran war live: Qatar, Saudi energy sites attacked; Riyadh says trust gone
text: Iran has launched retaliatory strikes against energy infrastructure in Qatar and Saudi Arabia following Israeli attacks on Iran's South Pars gasfield and naval assets. Saudi Arabia has declared that trust is gone, indicating a severe deterioration in diplomatic relations. This represents a dangerous escalation involving direct attacks on Gulf state energy facilities, threatening global energy security and potentially drawing additional regional powers into the Israel-Iran conflict. The targeting of critical energy infrastructure in multiple Gulf states marks a significant expansion of hostilities beyond the primary combatants.

title: Qatar expels Iranian attaches after LNG facility strike
text: CRITICAL: Qatar has expelled Iranian military and security attachés following Iranian missile strikes that caused extensive damage to the Ras Laffan LNG facility. This represents a major escalation in Gulf tensions, as Ras Laffan is one of the world's largest liquefied natural gas export facilities, critical to global energy markets. The Iranian attack on Qatari energy infrastructure and Qatar's diplomatic expulsion response signal a dangerous deterioration in regional stability with significant implications for global energy security and potential for wider conflict.

title: Funerals held for Afghans killed in Pakistani strike on Kabul hospital
text: CRITICAL - Pakistani military conducted an air strike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul, Afghanistan, resulting in multiple civilian casualties now being buried. This cross-border military action represents a significant escalation in Pakistan-Afghanistan tensions and marks a direct strike on the Afghan capital targeting what appears to be a civilian medical facility. The incident has major implications for regional stability and could trigger retaliatory actions or broader conflict between the two nations.

title: Iranians burn Trump, Netanyahu effigies at warship victim rally

This public demonstration of anti-US and anti-Israel sentiment suggests a significant incident involving Iranian naval assets has occurred, potentially escalating regional tensions between Iran and the US-Israel axis. title: US intelligence chief says Iran's regime 'intact' but 'degraded'
text: US intelligence chief testified before lawmakers that Iran's regime remains 'intact' but has been 'degraded' following recent conflict, with congressional members questioning whether the Islamic Republic posed an imminent threat that justified military action. This assessment suggests Iran retains governing capacity despite suffering significant damage to its military or strategic capabilities, raising critical questions about the justification and outcomes of US military engagement with Tehran.

title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
text: Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib has been killed in an Israeli air strike, with his death confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday. This represents a significant escalation in Israeli-Iranian hostilities, as the targeted killing of a cabinet-level intelligence official is an extraordinary act that will likely trigger substantial Iranian retaliation and could destabilize the broader Middle East region. The assassination of such a high-ranking security official marks a dramatic intensification of the shadow war between the two nations.
